narrative.headline: What 2023 Honda Hr-V owners report most
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints for this vehicle involves the rear windshield spontaneously shattering — sometimes while driving at highway speeds with no apparent trigger, and sometimes while the car is parked. A secondary cluster of complaints involves steering behavior, specifically the lane-keeping assist system acting in ways owners find unexpected or difficult to manage.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Owners report the rear windshield suddenly shattering or exploding while driving on the highway, with no nearby vehicles, overpasses, or road debris to account for the breakage. · 1 injured

issues.title: Visibility
issues.blurb: Owners report hearing an abrupt explosive sound while stopped in traffic, then discovering the rear windshield has shattered.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report that the lane-keeping assist system produces unexpected steering inputs that grow irritating, leading them to disable the feature. · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Owners report the rear window exploding spontaneously while the vehicle is parked and unoccupied, with no prior incident or impact.
